MASTER MOTION CALENDAR ON APR-16-2024 IN 206, APPEARANCE FOR DEFENDANTS/MOVING PARTIES. APPEARANCE FOR PLAINTIFF. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, ROD IWASHITA, LAVENA HOLMES, AND KAREN TAYLOR'S MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL IS GRANTED. THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE IS VACATED AND RE-SET FOR 2/18/25 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 206. DISCOVERY AND ALL PRE-TRIAL DEADLINES ARE ACCORDING TO THE NEW TRIAL DATE. THERE WILL BE NO FURTHER CONTINUANCE OF THE TRIAL DATE DUE TO THE GRANTING OF THIS MOTION FOR SUCH A LONG TIME PERIOD FROM THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ANNE-CHRISTINE MASSULLO; CLERK: D. LOK; COURT REPORTER: MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

Off Calendar Oct-05-2023 Continued To Jan-03-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-29-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, ROD IWASHITA, LAVENA HOLMES, KAREN TAYLOR'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

Sustained with leave to amend.

Second cause of action (harassment). The first amended complaint (FAC) fails to state facts sufficient to make out a claims for harassment. That individuals helped create a new job analysis does not alone suggest harassment, for two reasons. First, it is not clear what offensive message ("discriminatory intimidation, ridicule, and insult," as phrased in Kelly-Zurian v. Wohl Shoe Co., 22 Cal.App.4th 397, 409, quoting Harris v. Forklift Systems, Inc., 510 U.S. 17, 21 (1993)) was to be communicated to the plaintiff by this act. Second, it is not clear how that act was part of a "widespread pattern of bias," as authorities cited (mis-cited) by the plaintiff phrase the test. Roby v. McKesson Corp. (2009) 47 Cal.4th 686, 709.

These concerns are especially pertinent here because the acts in question seem to be "[p]ersonnel-related decisions involving discipline, performance evaluations, compensation, or job assignments [which] do not inherently constitute unlawful harassment." Cofer v. Parker-Hannifin Corporation (C.D. Cal. 2016) 194 F.Supp.3d 1014, 1018 (relying on state cases); see e.g., Reno v. Baird (1998) 18 Cal.4th 640, 645-646.

Seventh cause of action (fraud-concealment). As defendants note, a non-disclosure type of fraud requires facts to show a duty to disclose. E.g., Huy Fong Foods, Inc. v. Underwood Ranches, LP (2021) 66 Cal.App.5th 1112, 1121. No such facts are in the FAC. Nor does the FAC state facts showing the plaintiff was harmed by the failure to disclose.

Leave to amend. While there's no showing plaintiff can remedy the defects, it appears this demurrer is the first attack on these causes of action, and under those circumstances leave to amend is routinely granted. Amy's Kitchen, Inc. v. Fireman's Fund Ins. Co. (2022) 83 Cal.App.5th 1062, 1073; Tarrar Enterprises, Inc. v. Associated Indemnity Corp. (2022) 83 Cal.App.5th 685, 688.

The demurrers are sustained. A new complaint must be filed not later than October 13, 2023.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed form of order which repeats the Court's ruling verbatim.

Judge: Curtis Karnow, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/CK)
